behaviors.description: Generated reply references specific points from incoming email rather than producing generic response
behaviors.justification: A mock LLM can be scripted to return a contextual reply regardless of whether the real LLM would actually parse email details. The failure we're catching is 'the LLM ignores the context or produces generic text despite a well-constructed prompt.' Only a real LLM call with real email context reveals whether the system actually achieves contextual understanding or if the prompt engineering is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a real email thread with specific details (sender names, dates, product names, customer-specific concerns) to the full draft-generation pipeline with the real LLM. Verify the generated reply explicitly references at least 2-3 distinct details from the email (e.g., mentions a specific person named in the thread, addresses a particular question, acknowledges a unique concern). Run across 5+ different email threads to ensure it's consistent, not a fluke.

behaviors.description: Factual consistency check flags claims in draft that contradict knowledge base entries
behaviors.justification: The consistency checker is deterministic—it takes a draft and KB, outputs flags. We don't need a real LLM to test the checker; we provide test drafts with explicit contradictions. The failure mode is 'the checker's lookup or comparison logic is buggy.' Integration level catches this: real KB structure, real checker logic, test data with clear contradictions. Unit-level mocking could hide KB lookup bugs or data type mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a test knowledge base with concrete facts (e.g., 'Product X launched in Q3 2024', 'Customer Y is located in Toronto'). Create test drafts that contain explicit contradictions (e.g., 'Product X launched in Q2 2024', 'Customer Y is in Vancouver'). Run the consistency checker on each draft and verify it flags both contradictions. Verify it does NOT flag compatible or non-contradictory claims. Test edge cases: partial matches, case sensitivity, date formats.

behaviors.description: When knowledge base has no relevant entries, draft includes disclaimer rather than fabricating facts
behaviors.justification: The failure is 'the LLM hallucinates facts or presents unverified claims as certain when KB is empty.' Post-processing logic (Integration level) might add a disclaimer wrapper, but only a real LLM with real empty-KB context reveals whether the LLM itself resists hallucination. A mock LLM can be scripted to include a disclaimer; a real LLM might ignore the instruction under certain conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Provide an email thread on a topic (e.g., a new internal initiative, or a niche product detail) and ensure the KB contains zero entries relevant to that topic. Call the LLM with instructions to generate a reply and explicitly disclaim any unverified claims. Verify the draft includes explicit disclaiming language (e.g., 'I don't have information about...', 'Based on available data, I cannot confirm...') rather than stating uncertain claims as fact. Verify the draft does not assert facts that cannot be verified. Run across 5+ topics to ensure consistent behavior.

behaviors.description: Approval workflow blocks sending until user explicitly approves; no draft is sent automatically
behaviors.justification: The failure is 'draft is sent without approval approval check being enforced.' This could be a logic bug (approval flag not checked) or a workflow wiring bug (send called before approval). Integration level tests the full workflow with real approval logic and draft state management. We can mock the external email sending service itself (not a dependency we need to verify for this behavior).
behaviors.test_description: Generate a draft and place it in the approval state. Attempt to trigger a send without approval (via API call or UI action). Verify the send fails, either with an error response or by confirming the message is not queued for delivery. Explicitly approve the draft. Verify that only after approval does a send request succeed and the draft is marked as sent/delivered. Test both code path (approval status checked) and integration path (send endpoint rejects unapproved drafts).

behaviors.description: Full sequence works end-to-end: read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval
behaviors.justification: Individual behavior tests (1-4) don't catch failures where one step's output breaks the next step's input format, or where the LLM's specific draft content triggers an unexpected failure in the consistency checker, or where approval UI fails to render a particular draft structure. Only a real end-to-end test with actual LLM output flowing through all steps catches integration failures and emergent issues.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a realistic email thread (e.g., customer inquiry with multiple questions, internal request with specific context). Execute the full workflow: (1) read and parse the email thread, (2) generate a draft via LLM using the parsed context, (3) run the factual consistency check on the generated draft, (4) present the draft and consistency flags in the approval UI. Verify each step completes without error, the draft is readable and actionable, consistency flags are displayed correctly, and the user can approve the draft. Confirm the approved draft transitions to 'ready to send' state.
